task_completion.description: Navigate to the GCompris settings menu
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GCompris application open with the configuration/settings menu displayed (typically accessed via a wrench or gear icon).
In the settings menu, audio options (such as voices and effects) are visually toggled on or selected.

task_completion.description: Restrict the difficulty filter to levels 1-2
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the settings menu, the difficulty filter (represented by stars 1 through 6) is adjusted so that only levels 1 and 2 are active or highlighted.
In the settings menu, the virtual keyboard setting is visually toggled on or selected.

task_completion.description: Apply changes and return to the main menu
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshots show the GCompris main menu (showing activity categories, sun/stars motif), confirming the settings menu was closed and changes applied.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual GCompris GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual GCompris graphical interface being used to verify or change settings, rather than just a text editor modifying configuration files.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the application requirement using only terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must open the GCompris application to reach the main menu, not just run bash commands to edit ~/.config/KDE/gcompris-qt.conf and exit.

integrity.description: Settings were genuinely modified in the interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the progression of settings being changed from their default states (e.g., difficulty changing from 1-6 stars to 1-2 stars).

privileged_info_for_vlm: GCompris difficulty levels are represented by 1 to 6 stars; the task requires the filter to be set to exactly 1-2 stars. Audio feedback involves enabling voices and effects. The virtual keyboard is a specific toggle in the settings. The final required state is the GCompris main menu, meaning the settings panel must be closed after configuration.
